title: Credential Mosaic Attack: Valid Identity Fragments Never Previously Combined
narrative: Fraudster assembles a transaction using legitimate identity components that individually pass validation but have never been associated together in the cardholder's transaction history. The email address is valid for the card, the billing address exists in the card's profile, and the device passes basic checks, but these elements have never appeared in the same transaction before. Card-level counters show the card operating across an unusually diverse set of identity combinations compared to the cardholder's established pattern. Each identity fragment validates independently, creating a patchwork credential set that mimics legitimate multi-device or multi-location usage but lacks the consistent identity pairing patterns of genuine cardholders. The fraud surfaces through engineered features detecting novel identity-tuple combinations and elevated card-counter diversity metrics.
indicators: C1 > 45 indicating elevated card transaction counter in rolling window
card1_distinct_addr1 > 8 showing card used with many different billing addresses
C14 > 6 (p90) indicating card associated with multiple distinct email addresses
D1 = 0 and D2 > 90 showing new device-card pairing but established device age
V307 > 0.72 or V308 > 0.68 (Vesta engineered features flagging identity-tuple novelty)
C11 > 20 (p90) showing card used across many distinct transaction contexts
TransactionAmt between 70-180 (p25-p75 range, behaviorally unremarkable amount)
indicator_explanations: Card shows far more activity than typical cardholder baseline in recent time window
Same card number appearing with an unusually high count of different billing addresses
Card has been paired with multiple email addresses beyond normal cardholder pattern
Device has never processed this card before despite device having transaction history
Engineered risk features detect that this specific combination of identity elements is novel
Card counter reveals usage across diverse merchant or product categories
Transaction amount falls within normal spending range masking the credential abuse
summary: Valid email, billing address, and card details all check out individually, but this specific combination of identity elements has never been seen together before. Card counters reveal the card is being used across an abnormally diverse set of identity contexts.
reasoning: Legitimate cardholders exhibit consistent identity-pairing patterns across transactions. When valid identity fragments appear in novel combinations while card-level diversity metrics spike, it indicates a fraudster has obtained multiple valid identity elements for the card but doesn't know which ones actually go together, creating a mosaic of real data in an illegitimate arrangement.
